county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1822 from Houston, Jones, Monroe, Twiggs.
county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Macon. Major urban center — Macon was a Confederate manufacturing hub. Washington Memorial Library genealogy collection is excellent. Middle Georgia Archives at Mercer University. Extensive African American records: Freedmen's Bureau (NARA M1903), Macon city directories (1860s+), and Bibb County enslaved persons tax digests. Railroad records document many workers.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1822 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage licenses and returns from 1822. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.

source_profiles.source_name: Bibb County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, and appraisements from 1822.
Superior Court minutes include civil and criminal cases, name changes, naturalization, and guardianship records.
Tax digests listing enslaved persons by slaveholder; estate inventories with enslaved individuals named; bills of sale. Available on GA Archives microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Creek Nation Records — Bibb County Area
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-removal Creek records including census rolls, treaty records, and land lottery entries. Creek removal completed by 1830.
Confederate service records, Union occupation records, pension applications. Georgia Confederate pension files at GA Archives.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Cochran. Created 1912 from Pulaski — check Pulaski County for pre-1912 records. Small central GA agricultural county. Middle Georgia College (now MGA) archives hold some local records. FamilySearch microfilm. Bleckley County Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1912 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage licenses and returns from 1912. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.

source_profiles.source_name: Bleckley County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, and appraisements from 1912.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Nahunta. Created 1920 from Charlton, Pierce, and Wayne — very late formation means pre-1920 records in three parent counties. Okefenokee Swamp region. Limited early institutional resources. FamilySearch microfilm. Brantley County Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1920 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage licenses and returns from 1920. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.

source_profiles.source_name: Brantley County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, and appraisements from 1920.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Quitman. Created 1858 from Lowndes and Thomas. Brooks County Historical Society. Plantation economy — significant enslaved persons records in GA Archives. Quitman has well-preserved downtown with historical records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1858 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage licenses and returns from 1858. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.

source_profiles.source_name: Brooks County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, and appraisements from 1858.
Tax digests listing enslaved persons by slaveholder; estate inventories with enslaved individuals named; bills of sale. Available on GA Archives microfilm.

county_metadata.record_losses: Early records lost; fire and deterioration
county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Pembroke. Original 1777 county — one of GA's eight original counties from colonial parishes. Early records largely lost. Fort Stewart/Hunter Army Airfield (est. 1940) — military records affect many families. Check Chatham County for colonial-era records. GA Archives has some surviving Bryan County records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1793 to present. Some gaps due to Early records lost; fire and deterioration.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Colonial-era parish registers, vestry minutes, and related records. Coverage varies by parish.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some colonial records survive; many lost to deterioration and fire

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax digests list heads of household, property, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax digests often survive when other records are lost — many at GA Archives on microfilm

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Statesboro. 1856 fire destroyed early records. Georgia Southern University special collections hold Bulloch County materials. Significant agricultural community (Sea Island cotton, later tobacco). FamilySearch microfilm from 1856 restart. Bulloch County Historical Society has family files.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Some gaps due to 1856 fire destroyed early records.
Marriage licenses and returns from 1856. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Waynesboro. Original 1777 county from colonial St. George Parish. Various record losses pre-1800 — check GA Archives for surviving colonial records. Large plantation county — extensive enslaved persons records. Burke County Museum. Midville and Waynesboro community records.

Marriage licenses and returns from 1777. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Jackson. Created 1825 from Henry and Monroe. Indian Springs Hotel and Treaty site (1825 Treaty of Indian Springs with Creek Nation). Butts County Historical Society. FamilySearch microfilm. Rural piedmont county. Check Henry and Monroe for pre-1825 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1825 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage licenses and returns from 1825. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Morgan. Records lost in fire. Created 1854 from Baker and Early. Small southwest GA agricultural county. Limited institutional resources — GA Archives microfilm essential. Plantation economy with significant enslaved population pre-1865. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1854 to present. Some gaps due to Records lost in fire.
Marriage licenses and returns from 1854. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.

county_metadata.parent_county: St. Mary and St. Thomas Parishes
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1777 from St. Mary and St. Thomas Parishes.
county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Woodbine. Original 1777 county — colonial era records. Coastal county with plantation history. Cumberland Island National Seashore has Carnegie family records. St. Marys has some of GA's oldest surviving records. Naval Submarine Base Kings Bay (1978+) military records. Camden County Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1777 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage licenses and returns from 1777. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.
